---
title: "Homework week 9"
author: "Hidde Poel"
date: "2026-02-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

```{r import_libs, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=TRUE, cache=TRUE}
library(tidyverse)
library(compmus)

```

```{r make_timbre_matrix, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=TRUE, cache=TRUE}

timbre_csv <- read_csv("./reigniteOriginalMFCC.csv")

timbre_selfsim <- timbre_csv |>
  compmus_wrangle_timbre() |> 
  filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L) |> 
  mutate(timbre = map(timbre, compmus_normalise, "euclidean")) |>
  compmus_self_similarity(timbre, "cosine") |>
  filter(!is.na(d)) |> 
  mutate(d = d / max(d), type = "Timbre")
```

```{r make_chromagram_matrix, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=TRUE, cache=TRUE}

chromagram_csv <- read_csv("./reigniteOriginalChromagram.csv")

chromagram_selfsim <- chromagram_csv |>
  compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
  filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L) |> 
  mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |>
  compmus_self_similarity(pitches, "manhattan") |>
  filter(!is.na(d)) |> 
  mutate(d = d / max(d), type = "Chroma")
```

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the

output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r self-similarity, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=TRUE, cache=TRUE}


bind_rows(
  timbre_selfsim,
  chromagram_selfsim
) |>
  mutate() |>
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = xstart + xduration / 2,
      width = 100 * xduration,
      y = ystart + yduration / 2,
      height = 100 * yduration,
      fill = d
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  coord_fixed() +
  facet_wrap(~type) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(guide = "none") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "", y = "")


```
